claimText: The Beas River originates at Beas Kund near Rohtang Pass and joins the Sutlej River at Harike in Punjab.
evidenceExcerpt: Beas River originates at Beas Kund (Rohtang Pass) and joins the Sutlej at Harike, Punjab.
examUse: Statement-based question on the Indus river system and its tributaries.
distractorHooks: Joins the Ravi River instead of the Sutlej
Originates at Bara Lacha La instead of Rohtang Pass

claimText: The Chenab River is formed by the confluence of the Chandra and Bhaga rivers and is the largest tributary of the Indus River.
evidenceExcerpt: Chenab River is formed by Chandra and Bhaga rivers and is the largest tributary of the Indus.
examUse: Statement-based question on river origins and the largest tributaries of major river systems.
distractorHooks: Formed by the Nubra and Shyok rivers
Sutlej is the largest tributary of the Indus

claimText: The Subansiri River, also known as the Gold River, is the largest tributary of the Brahmaputra River.
evidenceExcerpt: Subansiri River, known as the Gold River, is the largest tributary of the Brahmaputra.
examUse: Statement-based question on the Brahmaputra river system.
distractorHooks: Lohit or Teesta is the largest tributary of the Brahmaputra

claimText: The Mandovi or Mahadayi River originates in Bhimgad in the Western Ghats, flows through Goa, Karnataka, and Maharashtra, and features the Dudhsagar waterfall.
evidenceExcerpt: Mandovi/Mahadayi River originates in Bhimgad (Western Ghats), flows through Goa, Karnataka, and Maharashtra, and features the Dudhsagar waterfall.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching rivers to their associated waterfalls or the states they flow through.
distractorHooks: Features Jog Falls instead of Dudhsagar Falls

claimText: The Subarnarekha River flows through Jharkhand, Odisha, and West Bengal, and hosts the Hundru Falls.
evidenceExcerpt: Subarnarekha River flows through Jharkhand, Odisha, and West Bengal, and hosts the Hundru Falls.
examUse: Statement-based question on east-flowing peninsular rivers and their associated waterfalls.
distractorHooks: Hosts Chitrakote Falls instead of Hundru Falls

claimText: The Kopili River flows through Meghalaya and Assam and is designated as National Waterway 57 (NW-57).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching rivers to National Waterways or states.
